name: Cape Verde

colors.symbolism: the sea and the sky surrounding the archipelago; the infinite space
the effort and dedication of the people
the colour of the ten stars; harmony and progress

description: A blue field crossed by a horizontal band of three thin stripes — white, red, white — set below the centre line. Ten yellow five-pointed stars are arranged in a circle on the hoist side, centred on the white-red-white band.
history: Adopted on 22 September 1992, replacing the 1975 independence flag (a horizontal red-yellow-green design with a black star, modelled on the joint Cape Verde / Guinea-Bissau flags of the PAIGC liberation movement). The new design followed the country's transition to multiparty democracy in 1991 and was meant to assert a national identity distinct from Guinea-Bissau, with which Cape Verde had been politically associated since independence.

features.symbolism: blue for the sea and the sky surrounding the islands, white for peace, red for the effort and dedication of the people

features.role: ten yellow five-pointed stars in a circle
features.symbolism: the ten main islands of the archipelago united as one nation
